creator: grahamperrin@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Philip Paeps from comment #2)

> … The freebsd-update build process only rebuilds the kernel when the 
> fixed vulnerability is in the kernel binary.  If the vulnerability is 
> in a module, only that module is rebuilt. …

Maybe worth noting that for pkgbase, each kernel package 
includes modules. 

Currently at <https://pkg.freebsd.org/FreeBSD:14:amd64/base_release_1/>,: 

* six of six FreeBSD-kernel⋯ packages are at patch level 6

* a total of eighteen packages are at this level.

From what's in the wiki, I assume that there is no plan to make kernel 
packages more granular – that the Project will continue to provide, 
for example, the FreeBSD-kernel-generic package with modules therein. 

pkg-add(8) is relevant, 

<https://man.freebsd.org/cgi/man.cgi?query=pkg-add&sektion=8&manpath=freebsd-ports>

----

Kernels aside: users should refrain from adding packaged FreeBSD-runtime 
without the broader pkgbase context – because subsequent attempts to use 
freebsd-update will interpret packaged /usr/bin/uname as the administrator 
having chosen pkgbase – instead of freebsd-update – as the future method 
for updates to any other packaged part of the operating system. 
856e158dc4aa5e43f0f15fcd3089fc0d71861b0e, 2024-11-02.

HTH
